text.content:
The ghost-spirit Zellara draws the PCs together with a harrow reading and sends them to kill crime-lord Gaedren Lamm, who hides in the Old Fishery with his thugs, his pet crocodile Gobblegut, and his enslaved orphans. The PCs recover Queen Ileosa's stolen brooch, Gaedren's ledger, and Zellara's haunted harrow deck — and as they leave, the king dies and Korvosa erupts.

Party: 4 PCs, level 1 → 2 (milestone on clearing the fishery). System: PF2e Remaster (Foundry v14 / pf2e 8.2.0).

Run it like Gotham. Tell players up front that Gaedren is a connector, not a final boss — this avoids the classic 'Lamm is a Lamb' let-down. Day: 9 orphans toil, thugs patrol, A7 open. Night: doors lock, 26 orphans sleep in A8, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo} roams (alarm). The orphans are non-combatants; rescuing them is the point.

First Harrowing (CHG-0010): run Zellara's reading as foreshadowing (coming unrest; the PCs are fated heroes), then grant each PC a small pool of hero-point-style suit boons via @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{her harrow deck}.

Map setup. Open the @UUID[Scene.PuUGEVunRqjIWFOj]{Old Fishery scene} — its map-note pins already link to each area page below and its tokens reference the converted actors. Drop in a map (Racooze's CotCT battlemaps, or your own at assets/maps/01-old-fishery.webp), then confirm pin/token positions (currently staged — NEEDS GM REVIEW). Drag @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Crocodile} (rename 'Gobblegut') into A13 and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Guard Dog} ('Bloo') into A4/A8 from the pf2e compendium.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A. Old Fishery — p.17.

A two-floor fishery on pilings over the Jeggare River, on a 13-ft embankment (@Check[type:athletics|dc:15] to climb the slick bank). Doors: @Check[type:thievery|dc:15] to pick; the brass key bypasses all. Standing hazards: @UUID[Actor.6y55S5DpXqnF40WL]{Slippery Boardwalk} (A3) and @UUID[Actor.xMhIQZS6GokTsulZ]{Rotten Ship Deck} (A9) — both can dump a PC into @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{the shark's} water (A12).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A1 — p.18.

Locked double doors (@Check[type:thievery|dc:15]); a loud pick or a knock brings @UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin} from A6.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A2 — p.18.

A 15-ft dock with slurry barrels; riverside stairs. A7 doors open by day; the A8 door is always locked (@Check[type:thievery|dc:15]).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A3 — p.18-20.

The @UUID[Actor.6y55S5DpXqnF40WL]{Slippery Boardwalk} hugs the south wall 13 ft above the river. A Step is safe; moving fast or fighting risks a fall into the shark water. The A6 door is locked.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A4 — p.19.

Under the desk sleeps @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo}, Yargin's cur (use Guard Dog; rename the token). He attacks strangers on sight and his barks rouse the fishery.

E1 · Bloo

Trivial · 20 XP

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo (Guard Dog −1)}

Silence him fast (Stealth/Diplomacy to avoid, or a quick kill) before the barks alert the others.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A5 — p.20.

The thugs' bunkroom; they distrust each other and keep no valuables here.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A6 — p.19-21.

@UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin Balko} works here. A hidden trapdoor (@Check[type:perception|dc:18] to spot; barred — @Check[type:athletics|dc:17] to force) drops to A13, a flanking route onto the boss. Yargin holds the brass key.

E2 · Yargin

Low · 40 XP

  • @UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin Balko (1)}

Opens with thrown Acid Flasks, then light crossbow. Flees down the trapdoor to warn Gaedren if losing.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A7 — p.20-22.

4 orphans toil under @UUID[Actor.qH96VshE6CZqfajv]{Hookshanks}, who hides among them (@Check[type:perception|dc:17] to pick out). Slippery floor (@Check[type:acrobatics|dc:15] if moving fast). Locked cash cabinet (@Check[type:thievery|dc:15]).

E3 · Hookshanks + orphans

Low · 40 XP

  • @UUID[Actor.qH96VshE6CZqfajv]{Hookshanks Gruller (1)}
  • 4× @UUID[Actor.UQr3ApF78GW7eghw]{Lamm's Lamb} (non-combatant)

Rally the orphans (@Check[type:diplomacy|dc:15]); Hookshanks can surrender and inform.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A8 — p.21-22.

Main slurry floor (8-ft vat, catwalks, a hole to the river). @UUID[Actor.mHUhxeaalWkTU1h4]{Giggles} oversees 5 orphans by day (26 sleep here at night). Beware feeding PCs through the hole to @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{the shark}.

E4 · Giggles [+Bloo at night]

Low · 40–60 XP

  • @UUID[Actor.mHUhxeaalWkTU1h4]{Giggles (1)}
  • 5× @UUID[Actor.UQr3ApF78GW7eghw]{orphans} (non-combatant)

Giggles tries to capture PCs nonlethally, then fights to the death (Orc Ferocity).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A9 — p.23-24.

A derelict ship; the @UUID[Actor.xMhIQZS6GokTsulZ]{Rotten Ship Deck} foredeck collapses under Medium+ weight (drop to A11). Stairs to the spider nest.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A10 — p.24.

Aft cabin lair of a single @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{drain spider} that lunges at the first to enter.

E5 · Drain Spider

Trivial · 20 XP

  • @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{Drain Spider (−1)}

Repelled by the vermin repellent looted in A13.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A11 — p.24.

Four more @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{drain spiders} infest the hold. Hull secret door (@Check[type:perception|dc:17]; +2 if @Check[type:survival|dc:15] tracked Gaedren) leads to A12 and the boss.

E6 · Drain Spider nest

Moderate · 80 XP (trivialized by vermin repellent)

  • 4× @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{Drain Spider (−1)}

Combine with the Rotten Deck hazard above (A9).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A12 — p.24-25.

A floating walkway under the fishery; Gaedren's escape skiffs are tied here. The small (2½-ft) door (@Check[type:thievery|dc:17]) leads to A13.

E7 · Jigsaw Shark

Low · 40 XP · avoidable

  • @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{Jigsaw Shark (1)}

Attacks anyone in the water; only leaps onto the walkway if attacked first.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A13 — p.24-26.

A chilly chamber over a water pit; two 5-ft walkways cross it. Loot tables line the far side. Something large stirs in the dark water below.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Gobblegut} (use Crocodile) lurks in the pit; @UUID[Actor.RKfT6vJ5guinSBjo]{Gaedren Lamm} sorts loot across it. Reaching him means crossing the gator's water. He opens with Spur the Beast (enrage Gobblegut), snipes, Nimble Dodges, and flees to the A12 skiffs at ≤8 HP.

E8 · BOSS — Gaedren + Gobblegut

SEVERE · 120 XP @ level 1 (Moderate @ level 2)

  • @UUID[Actor.RKfT6vJ5guinSBjo]{Gaedren Lamm (2)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Gobblegut (Crocodile 2)}

Scaling: 3 PCs → Weak Crocodile; 5–6 PCs → Elite Crocodile and/or add a Drain Spider from the pool. If Yargin reached Gaedren, start with Gobblegut pre-enraged.

On the tables: @UUID[Item.ymuRwMg6WfWIwkne]{darkwood coffer + 20 shiver + 7 vermin repellent}.

⚠ Track: leaving Gaedren's body here → Rolth animates it in the Dead Warrens (D13). See @UUID[.zceSTiompH1bHFDc]{Conversion Notes}.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A14 — p.26-28.

Gaedren's filthy quarters. Strongbox (rusty iron key, or @Check[type:thievery|dc:15]) holds @UUID[Item.iNQaVcbkSM0VPXsa]{Queen Ileosa's Brooch}. A hatbox holds Zellara's severed head (@Check[type:medicine|dc:14]: dead for weeks — the reveal) and @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{her harrow deck}. Atop the strongbox: @UUID[Item.I7HQNtjI9yPvQ4fA]{Gaedren's coded ledger}.

Full parcel on the @UUID[.svCjticTit7fdDrF]{Treasure} page.

• @UUID[Actor.RKfT6vJ5guinSBjo]{Gaedren Lamm} (L2) — A13 boss · @UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin Balko} (L1) — A6 · @UUID[Actor.qH96VshE6CZqfajv]{Hookshanks Gruller} (L1) — A7 · @UUID[Actor.mHUhxeaalWkTU1h4]{Giggles} (L1) — A8
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Gobblegut} = Crocodile (L2) · @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo} = Guard Dog (L−1) · @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{Drain Spider} (L−1) · @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{Jigsaw Shark} (L1) · @UUID[Actor.UQr3ApF78GW7eghw]{Lamm's Lambs} (non-combatant)
  • Zellara — the harrow-spirit guide, bound to @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{her deck} (story NPC, no stat block).

Build rationale: reports/npc_monster_conversion_report.md.

Rebuilt to PF2e level 1→2 (PF1e gp discarded; CHG-0008). Full table: reports/treasure_report.md.

  • @UUID[Item.b04PLWVkmm3RM4nm]{+1 Dagger (Gaedren's)} — boss loot · +1 armor potency (Gaedren's padded) · A14 hoard → 1st-rank scroll + ~50 gp gems.
  • @UUID[Item.dK4hnpH7s67RYnNI]{Garnet} (~8 gp) · @UUID[Item.ymuRwMg6WfWIwkne]{Coffer + vermin repellent} (~12 gp) · 2× Acid Flask + Thunderstone (Yargin) · 3× minor Healing Potion (Giggles) · ~30 gp coin.
  • @UUID[Item.I7HQNtjI9yPvQ4fA]{Coded ledger} → Guard bounty 15 gp (30 w/ cipher key).

Story items: @UUID[Item.iNQaVcbkSM0VPXsa]{Queen's Brooch} (launches the queen's audience + reward), @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{Zellara's Harrow Deck} (the Harrow subsystem). Don't restore PF1e gp.

Mirrors conversion_change_log.md (CHG-0101..0108). Story preserved; no approval-gated changes here.

  • Gaedren rebuilt as a credible Severe (with Gobblegut as the real threat) — fixes the 'Lamm is a Lamb' anticlimax + PF2e solo math. Added Spur the Beast / Nimble Dodge; kept Limping Gait. ⚠ Confirm Severe @L1 vs run @L2.
  • Gobblegut/Bloo referenced as official Crocodile/Guard Dog.
  • DCs re-derived to PF2e level-based/simple (locked-door spam collapsed).
  • Treasure rebuilt to PF2e level 1→2 (CHG-0008).
  • Drain Spider Venom: PF1e Con-drain → PF2e 1-stage poison + enfeebled.
  • Harrowing → hero-point-style suit pool (CHG-0010).
  • Orphans = explicit non-combatants (Rally/Cower); never an XP-for-kill source.
  • Forward hooks: leave Gaedren's body → Rolth zombie (D13); Brooch → queen's audience; deck → recurring Harrowings.
